kings_guild.name: The King's Guild
kings_guild.description: Master artisans of the King's Guild each ply a different trade in a different quarter of the city. Work out who's who.
clues: Beatrix, Potter, Tapestries, and Riverside belong to different artisans.
If someone in the Clothiers' Guild goes with Oldwall, then someone in the Market Ward goes with Fletcher.
11 coins goes with at least three of Wulfric, Tanner, Tapestries, and Millpond.
Matilda goes with at least two of Tanner, Barrels, Highrow, and 9 coins.
11 coins goes with Riverside if and only if Boots does not go with Tanner.
Either Horseshoes goes with Rowan or someone in the Market Ward goes with Rowan (but not both).
Between Fletcher and Tanner, one goes with Wulfric and the other with Highrow.
Between Horseshoes and Oldwall, one goes with Cooper and the other with 5 coins.
Exactly two of these are true: Tanner goes with Highrow; Weaver goes with 11 coins; Kingsford goes with 7 coins.
The Cooper's levy is higher than both Matilda and the artisan who makes Candles.

Suppose Horseshoes went with 3 coins — following the clues from there hits a contradiction, so it doesn't.
chain: Start by assuming Horseshoes goes with 3 coins.
Horseshoes goes with 3 coins, so it can't also go with 5 coins.
Horseshoes goes with 3 coins, but Cooper doesn't, so Cooper doesn't go with Horseshoes.
…but now the clue “Between Horseshoes and Oldwall, one goes with Cooper and the other with 5 coins” can no longer hold — a contradiction.
So Horseshoes does not go with 3 coins.

fishing.name: The Fishing Derby
fishing.description: Anglers at the lake-country derby each landed a different fish from a different pond on a different lure. Work out who caught what, and where.
clues: Cody, Catfish, Wobbler, and Pinehollow belong to different catches.
If someone in the Catfish Family goes with Mossbank, then someone in the North Watershed goes with Bluegill.
6 lb goes with at least three of Wade, Crappie, Wobbler, and Grayreach.
Mabel goes with at least two of Crappie, Crankbait, Birchpool, and 5 lb.
6 lb goes with Pinehollow if and only if Spinnerbait does not go with Crappie.
Either Swimbait goes with Royce or someone in the North Watershed goes with Royce (but not both).
Between Bluegill and Crappie, one goes with Wade and the other with Birchpool.
Between Swimbait and Mossbank, one goes with Bass and the other with 3 lb.
Exactly two of these are true: Crappie goes with Birchpool; Perch goes with 6 lb; Foxglen goes with 4 lb.
The angler who landed the Bass' weight is higher than both Mabel and the angler using the Spoon.
